name: anchor
description: P8-Lean 50K baseline checkpoint (Seed 42, GTX 1650). Frozen anchor for all comparisons.
notes: Frozen anchor. Trained on GTX 1650 with batch_size=64. Known issues: aux speed/role heads at 0% accuracy, switch prediction 37-48% vs move prediction 72-75%.

name: window5
description: Increase context window from 2 to 5 turns
hypothesis: More battle history gives the model richer context for predicting the next action, especially for switches that depend on HP/status trends
notes_summary: Timed out at epoch 6/10 with val_acc=0.498. Data loading took 12min of 30min budget. Need Tier 2.

name: window5_t2
description: Increase context window from 2 to 5 turns (Tier 2 for proper training)
hypothesis: More battle history gives the model richer context. AR-001 timed out at epoch 6 still improving. Need full training to converge.
notes_summary: Never ran - session interrupted before training started. Retry as new experiment.

name: full_100k
description: Full dataset (100K battles) with A40 throughput settings
hypothesis: More training data (80K vs 50K battles) should improve generalization, especially for rarer switch patterns
expected_impact: Top-1 +0.5-2pp from more diverse training data

name: window5_50k_t2
description: Window 5 with 50K battles, Tier 2 for full convergence
hypothesis: Expanding context from 2 to 5 turns gives the model history of HP trends, status, and previous actions - critical for switch prediction (currently 42.75%). AR-001 showed improvement trajectory at epoch 6 before timeout.
notes_summary: Window 5 KILL: 55.10% Top-1 (-8.11pp). Switch accuracy catastrophic at 19.4% (anchor 42.8%). Model too small for longer context.

name: switch_weight_2x
description: 2x class weight on switch actions in policy loss
hypothesis: Switch accuracy is 42.75% vs 74.17% for moves. Upweighting switch loss 2x forces the model to allocate more capacity to distinguishing correct switch targets. Switches are 35% of actions so a 5pp switch improvement = ~1.75pp overall.
notes_summary: Switch 2x: 61.85% Top-1 (-1.36pp). Switch acc +13.3pp but move acc -9.3pp. 2x too aggressive, try 1.5x.

name: switch_weight_1.5x
description: 1.5x class weight on switch actions - finding the sweet spot
hypothesis: AR-005 showed 2x switch weight improved switch acc +13.3pp but degraded moves -9.3pp. 1.5x should find a better balance: moderate switch gains with less move cost, potentially beating anchor overall.
notes_summary: Switch 1.5x: 61.68% Top-1 (-1.53pp). Move acc 69.2% (-5pp), switch acc 47.6% (+4.8pp). Switch weighting hurts Top-1 at all weights tested.

name: baseline_a40
description: Reproduce anchor config on A40 to establish true baseline
hypothesis: All experiments used batch_size=256, anchor used 64. Need to verify if batch size change alone explains the ~1.5pp gap. This baseline establishes the A40 reference point.
expected_impact: Should match anchor 63.21% if hardware/bf16 is neutral
notes_summary: Baseline A40: timed out at epoch 11. Eval: 56.68% Top-1 (undercooked). Confirms batch_size=64 needs 30 epochs.

name: batch256_lr_scaled
description: batch_size=256 with linear-scaled LR (4e-4) to match batch_size=64 dynamics
hypothesis: Previous batch_size=256 experiments may converge worse due to 4x fewer gradient updates with same LR. Linear scaling rule: multiply LR by batch_size ratio (256/64=4x) and reduce warmup proportionally. This should recover the anchor's convergence behavior while being 4x faster per epoch.
notes_summary: Scaled LR: 62.10% Top-1 (-1.11pp). Move acc 75.2% (+1pp) but switch acc 37.7% (-5.1pp). Closer to anchor but still below.

name: lr_scaled_ls05
description: Scaled LR (4e-4) + label smoothing 0.05 to reduce overfitting
hypothesis: AR-008 showed 3.5pp train-val gap at convergence. Label smoothing 0.05 should reduce overfitting and improve generalization, potentially closing the 1.11pp gap to anchor.
notes_summary: DIVERGED: loss=inf at epoch 2. LR 4e-4 + label_smoothing 0.05 is unstable. Label smoothing needs lower LR.

name: t1_baseline
description: Tier 1 baseline: batch=256, LR=4e-4, 25K battles, 5 epochs. Reference for all Tier 1 experiments.
hypothesis: Establish fast baseline for Tier 1 comparisons. All Tier 1 experiments will be compared against this.

name: t1_dropout_005
description: Dropout 0.05 (down from 0.1)
hypothesis: Small model may be over-regularized. Less dropout = more effective capacity.

name: t1_aux_weight_0
description: Aux weight 0 (no auxiliary loss)
hypothesis: Aux head predicts only items (speed/role broken). Zero aux weight focuses all capacity on policy loss.
expected_impact: May improve or neutral — test if broken aux hurts

name: t1_dropout005_aux0
description: Combine two best signals: dropout=0.05 + aux_weight=0
hypothesis: Both dropout 0.05 (+0.48pp) and aux_weight 0 (+0.33pp) showed positive signal. Combining may stack.
expected_impact: Top-1 +0.5-1pp from stacking two positive signals

name: t1_seed_123
description: Different seed (123) to measure Tier 1 variance
hypothesis: Measure how much of Tier 1 results are noise vs signal. If variance is >1pp, many previous 'signals' are noise.

name: t1_dropout_0
description: Dropout 0.0 (no dropout at all)
hypothesis: Maximum model capacity. At Tier 1 scale with 25K data the model isn't really overfitting, so dropout may just hurt.
expected_impact: Top-1 +0.5-1pp if dropout is hurting at this scale

name: t2_deep_5L
description: Deep architecture: 5L/224d/4H (same width as anchor, more depth)
hypothesis: More depth adds representational capacity through more processing steps. Same width means less parameter growth than wider model.

name: t2_deep_6L
description: Deeper: 6L/224d/4H. 5L beat anchor — does more depth help further?
hypothesis: 5L gave +0.36pp. If depth is the key variable, 6L should improve further.

name: t2_candidate_head
description: Candidate-conditioned policy head: cross-attention scorer instead of pooled MLP. Move candidates use active pokemon + move slot embedding. Switch candidates use bench pokemon tokens directly.
hypothesis: Current pooled-MLP head treats switches as abstract slot IDs. Candidate head lets model reason about actual switch targets (specific bench pokemon) via cross-attention over encoder memory. Should dramatically improve switch accuracy (currently 37-48%).

name: t2_split_head
description: Split policy head with separate cross-attention for moves and switches
hypothesis: Separate move/switch scoring pathways reduce interference between action types, improving switch accuracy without hurting moves
expected_impact: Improve switch accuracy; maintain or improve Top-1 over candidate head (66.63%)

name: t2_split_move_identity
description: Split head with move-identity candidates using actual move ID embeddings
hypothesis: Move-identity candidates let the model score actual moves (Recover, Ice Punch) instead of abstract slot positions, improving move selection accuracy
expected_impact: Improve move accuracy and Top-1 over split head champion (67.41%)

name: t2_split_shuffle_moves
description: Split head champion + per-battle move slot shuffle augmentation
hypothesis: Per-battle move slot shuffling forces the model to learn move identities instead of slot positions, improving generalization and move accuracy
expected_impact: May initially lower Top-1 as slot shortcuts are removed, but should improve move selection quality and generalization. Expect improved accuracy once model learns actual move identity patterns.

name: t2_split_shuffle_identity
description: Split head + move shuffle augmentation + move-identity candidates
hypothesis: Move shuffle prevents slot memorization while move-identity candidates give the model a way to identify actual moves. Together they force the model to learn 'use Earthquake' instead of 'click slot 1'.
expected_impact: Should learn slower initially but generalize better. Key test: does the model's move probability follow the move identity when slots are swapped?

name: t2_shuffle_identity_100k_bs1024
description: Split head + shuffle + move identity with 4x compute: 100K battles, 40 epochs, batch=1024, LR=8e-4
hypothesis: More compute (100K battles, 40 epochs) with larger batch (1024) and sqrt-scaled LR (8e-4) gives the shuffle+identity model enough training to learn move-specific patterns
expected_impact: Close the gap between the move-identity model (55.09%) and the slot-based champion (67.41%) while maintaining correct move-identity generalization

name: t2_shuffle_id_100k_cosine80
description: Same as AR-023 but with cosine schedule calibrated for 80 epochs
hypothesis: Cosine schedule targeting 80 epochs keeps LR at ~50% of peak at epoch 40, preventing the LR death that wasted 5 epochs in AR-023
expected_impact: Improve over 58.33% by maintaining useful LR through all 40 epochs

name: t2_shuffle_id_100k_cosine80_v2
description: Same as AR-023 but with cosine schedule calibrated for 80 epochs
hypothesis: Cosine schedule targeting 80 epochs keeps LR at ~50% of peak at epoch 40, preventing the LR death that wasted 5 epochs in AR-023
expected_impact: Improve over 58.33% by maintaining useful LR through all 40 epochs
